journal.page_intro:
Suzanne Schwager is a member of the American Art Pottery Association
American Art Pottery Association
https://aapa.info › journal

about.hero_description: Suzanne continues to create decorative pottery similar to the work of turn of the 19th century American Art Pottery studios. Rookwood, Van Briggle and Newcomb College designs are her inspiration.
about.bio: Suzanne Schwager began her pottery career while studying ceramics at the University of California Santa Barbara. She was hired by Santa Barbara Ceramic Design to design and decorate art pottery that would be exhibited in various craft shows in the 1980's. One of these shows included the Smithsonian Institute's American Art Pottery Exhibit. She has exhibited her pottery in the Los Angeles Art Pottery Show and sells her pieces to private collectors. Suzanne creates her pottery in her home studio where she hand throws each piece on the potter's wheel. She fires the clay pieces after hand painting the individual ware.
about.origin_text_1: Suzanne's home studio in the Riviera district of Santa Barbara California is a beautiful inspiration for her designs. The views of the Santa Barbara marina, Spanish tiled homes, and scenic foothills are reminiscent of early California days. Her over glazes cast a subtle vintage tone over carefully drawn underglazes. Her travels to the countries of Italy, Greece, France, and Spain have brought a Mediterranean flair to her home pottery studio.
about.origin_text_2: After Suzanne completes a group of individual hand thrown pieces she will bring them to her small art gallery for display. The art gallery is located in the Arlington district in downtown Santa Barbara. Suzanne can be contacted by sending her a message through email located in the contact tab.

contact.intro: If you have questions about a piece, a commission, or a studio visit, send a note and we'll respond within a few days.
contact.intro_2: If you'd like to arrange a studio visit please get in touch close to the time that you'd like to come, rather than months or weeks ahead.
